task_updates.executor_notes: Ran `npx tsc --noEmit` successfully, then re-ran `npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/domains/media-lightbox src/tools/edit-images`, which passed with 42/42 files and 183/183 tests. This CLI environment does not provide an interactive browser for literal manual smoke, so I used the existing expanded Phase 2 automated coverage as the smoke substitute: `InlineEditView.test.tsx` exercises all six edit modes (`text`, `inpaint`, `annotate`, `reposition`, `img2img`, `upscale`) and verifies their generate/save handlers stay wired, while `useInlineEditState.test.ts` verifies prompt-backed state remains stable across mode changes at the hook boundary. The existing inline tests still verify the close button closes the inline editor and the variant controls continue to select, promote, and delete correctly. Full `npm test` still ends with the same unrelated repo-wide failure buckets already tracked outside this batch (19 failed files / 36 failed tests across 8 failed suites, 863 passed files / 4506 passed tests); none of those failures are in the media-lightbox or edit-images scopes touched here. Phase 2 validation is complete and the changes are ready to ship as their own PR.
commands_run: npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/domains/media-lightbox src/tools/edit-images

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed Phase 2 type-check is clean and the scoped `src/domains/media-lightbox` + `src/tools/edit-images` vitest run passes (42/42 files, 183/183 tests). This environment does not support an interactive browser smoke session, so I used the existing expanded inline-edit tests as the smoke substitute: `InlineEditView.test.tsx` verifies all six edit modes render and keep their generate/save handlers wired, `useInlineEditState.test.ts` verifies prompt-backed state persists across mode switches, and the existing inline tests still verify close-button wiring plus variant actions.
